question: In the segment where the camera angle shifts to an upward view towards the surface, how many sharks are visible swimming near the water surface? A. 1 B. 2 C. 3 D. 4
How many smaller fish are associated with the large shark in the segment where one is attached to its body and another swims beneath it?
What is the primary body color of the large shark seen swimming in the majority of the video clips? A. Tan B. Silver C. Grey D. Green
What entities are present and what are they doing?
canonical_answer: A large grey shark swims over a sandy seabed scattered with dark seagrass while smaller remora fish attach to its dorsal side or swim beneath it. A shark holds a small silver fish in its jaws while swimming close to the ocean floor. Multiple sharks are also seen swimming near the water surface in silhouette against the sky.

question: Which of the previously seen sharks is back on screen? A. large tan shark resting on the sandy ocean floor B. large shark with remora fish attached swimming over sandy seabed with seagrass C. large shark with mouth revealing sharp teeth swimming through blue water D. shark silhouette viewed from below against bright surface water

Did the video show BOTH a grey shark swimming from left to right AND two sharks silhouetted against the bright surface water? A. Only the grey shark swimming left to right was shown B. Only the two sharks silhouetted were shown C. Both the grey shark swimming left to right and the two sharks silhouetted were shown D. Neither was shown

In which spatial position is the large grey shark primarily described during the segment where its gill slits and small eye are visible? A. On the left side of the frame B. On the right side of the frame C. Near the water surface D. Close to the ocean floor

Where is one of the smaller fish located relative to the large grey shark in the segment where two smaller fish are associated with the shark? A. In its jaws B. On the dorsal side C. Beneath it D. Alongside it
What action is the large shark performing in this segment? A. Swimming with a remora attached B. Swimming over a sandy seabed C. Revealing rows of teeth
Describe the sequence of actions and interactions involving the sharks and other fish throughout the video.
canonical_answer: At 0s, a grey shark swims over a sandy seabed with its mouth open; at 6s, two remora fish appear attached to or swimming beneath the shark; at 8s, the shark holds a silver fish in its jaws while swimming; at 16s, a tan shark and a dark grey shark rest on the ocean floor with remoras nearby.

question: What specific action is the large grey shark performing in this clip? A. Resting on the sandy ocean floor B. Holding a small silver fish in its jaws C. Swimming in a group of three sharks D. Kicking up a cloud of sand from the seabed

In the segment where the sharks are seen on the sandy bottom without swimming, how does their behavior compare to the start? A. They transitioned from swimming to resting on the sandy floor B. They transitioned from resting to swimming near the surface C. They transitioned from swimming to hunting a small fish D. They transitioned from swimming to surfacing in a group
What marine life becomes visible in the water after the shark passes?
canonical_answer: A school of small silver fish swims above the sandy bottom.

question: What step came IMMEDIATELY before the shark was seen holding a fish in its jaws? A. Swimming in a group near the water surface B. Swimming with remora fish attached C. Swimming alone close to the ocean floor D. Holding a silver fish in its jaws
What type of prey is the large grey shark actively hunting in this footage? A. Squid B. Seals C. Crustaceans D. Unable to answer
What color is the wetsuit worn by the diver filming the sharks? A. Red B. Unable to answer C. Orange D. Purple

Given what is visible so far, could the large shark swim above the seagrass bed? A. No - the shark is silhouetted against the surface B. Yes - the shark and seagrass bed are both visible C. No - only open blue water is visible without a seabed D. No - the shark is swimming diagonally upwards away from the bottom
